title: bun cross-thread GC corruption: getHeapSnapshot copies a parent-VM JSC::Strong into a worker-thread lambda (PR #30185)
category: cross-thread data race / JSC handle thread-affinity violation (corrupts the parent VM's HandleSet under concurrent GC)
crash_site: JSC GC marking of the PARENT VM's strong handles — HandleSet::visitStrongHandles walking a SentinelLinkedList, null-deref at 0x10 (JSC internals). The crash is in correct GC code whose invariant was violated elsewhere.

ground_truth.decoy_fn: the JSC crash site (HandleSet::visitStrongHandles / SentinelLinkedList push/remove) which is CORRECT; the recent worker-lifetime rewrites (#29957/#29937) which look related but did NOT introduce it; BunV8HeapSnapshotBuilder (the worker's own concurrent GC) as a red herring
ground_truth.fix_summary: getHeapSnapshot captured a JSC::Strong<JSPromise> (in the parent VM's HandleSet) BY VALUE into cross-thread lambdas; JSC::Strong has no move ctor, so the copy/destroy ran HandleSet allocate/deallocate + SentinelLinkedList push/remove on the WORKER thread without the parent VM's lock, corrupting the list that the parent VM's GC marking concurrently walks. Fix: heap-allocate the Strong once on the parent thread, pass only the raw pointer through the worker lambdas (the worker never touches the parent HandleSet), and resolve+free on the parent side.

admission.note: monogram+haiku & baseline+haiku DISCRIMINATION round. Expected VERY HARD + discriminating: the crash is a null-deref deep in JSC GC marking of the PARENT VM's HandleSet (looks like a JSC bug / memory corruption with no origin), but the cause is a C++ binding copying a VM-bound JSC::Strong by value into a worker-thread lambda — a thread-affinity violation, with NO text bridge from crash to cause (the symptom never mentions snapshots). Live decoys: the correct JSC crash-site code, and the recent worker-lifetime-rewrite PRs the author explicitly exonerates. Obscure 2026 CI flake → low contamination.

provenance: oven-sh/bun PR #30185 'worker: fix cross-thread HandleSet race in getHeapSnapshot' (merged 2026-05-03; merge 0150c57051f2; pre-fix base 0561f87d42ce = the merge of #30196). Flaky CI segfault (panic at 0x10) on a parent-VM GC helper thread, decoded from a bun.report trace (build 50529). Root cause present since getHeapSnapshot was added; the worker-lifetime rewrites #29957/#29937 did NOT introduce it (explicit decoy). Fix touches JSWorker.cpp (cause) + Worker.cpp/.h (raw-pointer plumbing).
